name: apollo-fpga

metadata.name: apollo-fpga
metadata.summary: Python library and host tools for Apollo FPGA debug controllers.

documentation:
# Apollo FPGA Programmer / Debugger

Apollo is the on-board debugger and programmer on [Cynthion](https://greatscottgadgets.com/cynthion/). It is used to load gateware over USB onto Cynthion's FPGA. Alternatively it may be used as an on-board or external debugger for certain other FPGA platforms.

Apollo consists of two main parts: firmware for the on-board debug microcontroller and Python-based software for the host computer.

## Installing Host Software

If you have a Cynthion, Apollo host software is installed automatically as a dependency when you [install Cynthion software](https://cynthion.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting_started.html#cynthion-host-software-installation).

To explicitly install the apollo-fpga Python module and the `apollo` command-line tool or to upgrade them to the latest version run:
```
pip install --upgrade apollo-fpga
```

## Building and Installing Firmware

To upgrade Apollo firmware on a Cynthion it is typically not necessary to compile the firmware yourself. Instead follow [Upgrading Cynthion Device Firmware](https://cynthion.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting_started.html#updating-cynthion-microcontroller-firmware-and-fpga-configuration-flash).

To compile and install onto Cynthion run:

```
$ cd apollo/firmware
$ make APOLLO_BOARD=cynthion get-deps dfu
```

This will download dependencies, compile the firmware, and install it onto Cynthion with [pyfwup](https://github.com/greatscottgadgets/pyfwup) and [Saturn-V](https://github.com/greatscottgadgets/saturn-v).

Alternatively you can use variables to specify a different board or a fixed hardware revision:

```
$ cd apollo/firmware
$ make APOLLO_BOARD=cynthion BOARD_REVISION_MAJOR=1 BOARD_REVISION_MINOR=4 get-deps dfu
```

A specified revision will override automatic hardware revision detection which is supported on Cynthion r0.6 and above. You must specify the revision to compile firmware for a Cynthion older than r0.6.

Once installation is complete, LED A should activate, indicating that Apollo is running.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.gateware.advertiser.ApolloAdvertiser
documentation:
Gateware that implements an announcement signal for Apollo using the FPGA_ADV pin.

Used to tell Apollo that the gateware wants to use the CONTROL port.
Apollo will keep the port switch connected to the FPGA after a reset as long as this 
signal is being received and the port takeover is allowed.

I/O ports:
    I: stop -- Advertisement signal is stopped if this line is asserted.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.gateware.advertiser.ApolloAdvertiserRequestHandler
documentation:
Request handler for ApolloAdvertiser. 

Implements default vendor requests related to ApolloAdvertiser.

The returned format is the same as used in a nMigen platform file; and can be used to override

classes.name: apollo_fpga.support.bits.bits
documentation:
An immutable bit sequence, like `bytes` but for bits.

This bit sequence is ordered from LSB to MSB; this is the direction in which it is converted
to and from iterators, and to and from bytes. Note, however, that it is converted to and from
strings (which should be only used where a human-readable form is required) from MSB to LSB;
this matches the way integer literals are written, as well as values in datasheets and other
documentation.

documentation:
Emits a VCD file containing the ILA samples.

Parameters:
    filename      -- The filename to write to, or '-' to write to stdout.
    gtkw_filename -- If provided, a gtkwave save file will be generated that
                     automatically displays all of the relevant signals in the
                     order provided to the ILA.
    add_clock     -- If true or not provided, adds a replica of the ILA's sample
                     clock to make change points easier to see.

documentation:

    dump_filename -- The filename of the VCD that should be opened with this save.
    add_clock     -- True iff a clock signal should be added to the GTKW save.

modules.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5
documentation: ECP5 configuration.
This file is part of Apollo.
Copyright (c) 2020-2024 Great Scott Gadgets <info@greatscottgadgets.com>
SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause

classes.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5Programmer

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5Programmer.__init__
documentation:
Captures the common fields for all ECP5 programmers.

Paramters, all optional:
    cfg_pins -- A (potentially virtual) port containing the three CFG pins that select the
        device's configuration mode. See VirtualGPIOPort for an example of a class that
        might be used for this.
    init_pin -- A GPIOPin object that connects the device's INIT_N (program status) pin.
    program_pin -- A GPIOPin object that drives the device's PROGRAM_B (program trigger) pin.
    done_pin -- A GPIOPin object that reads the status of the FPGA's done pin.

If pins are not provided, their functionality (e.g. checking) will not be used, and it will
be up to the subclass to handle this. Subclasses may choose to make these required.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5Programmer._set_configuration_mode_pins
documentation:
Sets the configuration mode pins to the given binary-valued configuration mode.

Returns True if this method was able to set the configration pins to their relevant value,
or False otherwise.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5Programmer._restart_configuration_process
documentation:
Attempts to strobe the PROGRAM line, to re-start the configuration process.

Returns True iff we had the correct

inner_classes.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5Programmer.Opcode
documentation: Enumeration that describes the ECP5 configuration opcodes.

inner_classes.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5Programmer.FlashOpcode
documentation: Enumeration that describes the opcodes used to work with an ECP5's configuration flash.
Abstract base class for programming ECP5 FPGAs.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._execute_command
documentation:
Issue an ECP5 configuration command.

Parameters:
    opcode -- The opcode to issue.
    data_or_length -- A bytes-like object that contains the data to be sent; or an integer that contains
        the amount of data to be read.
    wait_for_completion -- If set, execution will block until the FPGA reports that the command is complete.
        Only necessary for long-running command.
    never_print -- True if this is a frequently-executed command (or a command called as part of this
        function's implementation). Prevents emission of verbose "what we're doing" text.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer.read_id
documentation: Returns the identification code for the attached ECP5.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer.part_name
documentation: Returns a descriptive name of the attached FPGA, if possible.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._validate_status
documentation: Checks the status register after reading a given command.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._perform_preconfiguration_tasks
documentation:
Handles any tasks that need to be executed prior to configuration.

Intended to allow subclasses a chance to add functionality. For example, a JTAG
subclass would want to issue an LSC_PRELOAD here.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._generate_bit_reversed_bitstream
documentation:
Generates a copy of the provided bitstream with the bits in each byte

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer.configure
documentation:
Configures the attached FPGA with the relevant bitstream file.

bitstream -- An bytes-like object containing the data to be configured into the FPGA. Any object that
    can be passed to bytearray's constructor is acceptable.

documentation: Restarts the configuration process; equivalent to toggling the PROGRAM_N pin.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._capture_part_id

Reads and stores the device's part ID.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._read_status

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._read_usercode

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer._device_is_busy

Attempts to read the FPGA's configuration flash's ID over JTAG.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5CommandBasedProgrammer.read_flash_uid

Attempts to read the FPGA's configuration flash's unique ID over JTAG.

Returns a 64-bit ID value.

documentation: Reads the contents of the attached FPGA's configuration flash.

Abstract base for ECP5 programmers that are command-centric (i.e. everything except Master SPI and Slave Serial.)
To implement, override (at least) _execute_command.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5SlaveSPI

Creates a new ECP5 Slave SPI (SSPI) configuration interface.

Parameters:

    spi_bus -- The SPI bus to use for programming. If omitted, the board's
        default SPI bus will be used.

See ECP5Programmer.__init__ for additional accepted arguments.

Issue an ECP5 configuration command.

Parameters:
    opcode -- The opcode to issue.
    data_or_length -- A bytes-like object that contains the data to be sent; or an integer that contains
        the amount of data to be read.
    wait_for_completion -- If set, execution will block until the FPGA reports that the command is complete.
        Only necessary for long-running command.
    never_print -- True if this is a frequently-executed command (or a command called as part of this
        function's implementation). Prevents emission of verbose "what we're doing" text.
Class that enables configuring ECP5 FPGAs via GreatFET boards.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5MasterSerialDirect

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5MasterSerialDirect.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new ECP5 Slave SPI (SSPI) configuration interface.

Parameters:
    board -- The GreatFET board to use for configuration.
    chip_select -- The GPIOPin to be used for chip select, or None to use the
        board's default.
    program_pin -- The GPIO pin to be used to control the FPGA's PROGRAM_N line.

See ECP5Programmer.__init__ for additional accepted arguments.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5MasterSerialDirect.trigger_reconfiguration
documentation: Triggers the target FPGA to reconfigure itself from its flash chip.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5MasterSerialDirect.program
documentation: Programs the target flash with the relevant bitstream; but does not trigger reconfiguration of the FPGA.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5MasterSerialDirect.configure
documentation: Programs the target flash, and then triggers the FPGA to reconfigure itself from it.

ECP5 Programmer class for programming the SPI flash used for Master SPI.

This variant expects we have a direct connection to the target flash, rather than e.g.
programming it through JTAG.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5_JTAGProgrammer

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5_JTAGProgrammer.__init__
documentation:
Parameters:
    board -- The GreatFET board to use for configuration.
    chain -- The JTAG chain used for programming.
        default SPI bus will be used.

See ECP5Programmer.__init__ for additional accepted arguments.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5_JTAGProgrammer._execute_command
documentation:
Issue an ECP5 configuration command.

Parameters:
   opcode -- The opcode to issue.
    data_or_length -- A bytes-like object that contains the data to be sent; or an integer that contains
        the amount of data to be read.
    wait_for_completion -- If set, execution will block until the FPGA reports that the command is complete.
        Only necessary for long-running command.
    never_print -- True if this is a frequently-executed command (or a command called as part of this
        function's implementation). Prevents emission of verbose "what we're doing" text.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5_JTAGProgrammer._enter_background_spi
documentation: Places the FPGA into background SPI mode; for e.g. programming a connected flash.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.ecp5.ECP5_JTAGProgrammer._background_spi_transfer
documentation: Performs a background SPI transfer, targeting the configuration flash.
Class that enables configuring ECP5 FPGAs over JTAG.

documentation: Attempts to create a JTAGDevice object that fits the provided IDCODE.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGDevice.supports_idcode
documentation:
Returns true iff this class supports the given IDCODE.

This default implementation uses SUPPORTED_IDCODES, but subclasses can override this
for more nuanced behavior.

documentation: Returns this device's IDCODE.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGDevice.description

Class representing a single device on a JTAG scan chain.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.__init__
documentation:
Creates a new JTAG scan-chain interface.

Paramters:
    board         -- the Apollo debugger we're working with.
    max_frequency -- the maximum frequency we should attempt scan out data with

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.initialize
documentation:
Starts use of a persistent JTAG connection.
When possible prefer using 'with' on this object.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.__enter__
documentation: Starts a new JTAG communication.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.__exit__
documentation: Terminates an active JTAG communication.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.set_frequency
documentation: Sets the operating frequency of future transactions on this JTAG chain.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.initialize_chain
documentation: Put the scan chain into its initial state, allowing fresh JTAG communications.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._receive_data_chunk
documentation: Performs a raw scan-in of data, and returns the result.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._pad_data_to_length
documentation: Pads a given data set to a given length, in bits.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._transmit_data
documentation: Performs a raw scan-out of data, discarding any result.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._scan_data
documentation: Performs a raw scan-in of data, and returns the result.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._reverse_bits_where_necessary
documentation:
Applies any neceessary bit-manipulations before/after a JTAG scan.

Used as some hardware can only send MSB->LSB, and not LSB->MSB.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._scan_data_chunk
documentation: Performs a raw scan-in of data, and returns the result.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._ensure_in_state
documentation:
Ensures the JTAG TAP FSM is in the given state.
If we're not; progresses the TAP FSM by pulsing TMS until we reach the relevant state.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.move_to_state
documentation:
Moves the JTAG scan chain to the relevant state.

Parameters:
    state_name: The target state to wind up in, as a string. States are accepted in the format
    defined in the JTAG SVF standard, and thus should be one of:
        "RESET", "IDLE", "DRSELECT", "DRCAPTURE", "DRSHIFT", "DREXIT1", "DRPAUSE",
        "DREXIT2", "DRUPDATE", "IRSELECT", "IRCAPTURE", "IRSHIFT", "IREXIT1", "IRPAUSE",
        "IREXIT2", "IRUPDATE"

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._shift_while_in_state
documentation: Shifts data through the chain while in the given state.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._validate_response
documentation: Validates the response provided by a _shift_while_in_state call, in the traditional JTAG SVF form.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.shift_data
documentation:
Shifts data through the scan-chain's data register.

Parameters:
    tdi    -- The bits to be scanned out via TDI. Can be a support.bits() object, a string of 1's and 0's,
              an integer, or bytes. If this is an integer or bytes object, the length argument must be provided.
              If omitted or None, a string of all zeroes will be used,
    length -- The length of the transaction to be performed, in bits. This can be longer than the TDI data;
              in which case the transmission will be padded with zeroes.
    tdo    -- The expected data to be received from the scan operation. If this is provided, the read result
              will be compared to this data (optionally masked by mask), and an exception will be thrown if
              the data doesn't match this value. Designed to behave like the SVF TDO field.
    mask   -- If provided, the given tdo argument will be masked, such that only bits corresponding to a '1'
              in this mask argument are considered when checking against 'tdo'. This is the behavior defiend
              in the SVF standard; see it for more information.
    ignore_response -- If provided; the returned response will always be empty, and tdo and mask will be ignored.
                       This allows for slight a performance optimization, as we don't have to shuttle data back.
    byteorder       -- The byteorder to consider the tdi value in; if bytes are provided.

Returns the bits read, or None if the response is ignored.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.shift_instruction
documentation:
Shifts data through the chain's instruction register.

Parameters:
    tdi    -- The bits to be scanned out via TDI. Can be a support.bits() object, a string of 1's and 0's,
              an integer, or bytes. If this is an integer or bytes object, the length argument must be provided.
              If omitted or None, a string of all zeroes will be used,
    length -- The length of the transaction to be performed, in bits. This can be longer than the TDI data;
              in which case the transmission will be padded with zeroes.
    tdo    -- The expected data to be received from the scan operation. If this is provided, the read result
              will be compared to this data (optionally masked by mask), and an exception will be thrown if
              the data doesn't match this value. Designed to behave like the SVF TDO field.
    mask   -- If provided, the given tdo argument will be masked, such that only bits corresponding to a '1'
              in this mask argument are considered when checking against 'tdo'. This is the behavior defiend
              in the SVF standard; see it for more information.
    ignore_response -- If provided; the returned response will always be empty, and tdo and mask will be ignored.
                       This allows for slight a performance optimization, as we don't have to shuttle data back.
    byteorder       -- The byteorder to consider the tdi value in; if bytes are provided.

Returns the bits read, or None if the response is ignored.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.run_test
documentation:
Places the device into the RUNTEST/IDLE (or provided) state, and pulses the JTAG clock.

Paraameters:
    cycles -- The number of cycles for which the device should remain in the given state.
    from_state -- The state in which the cycles should be spent; defaults to IDLE.
    end_state -- The state in which the device should be placed after the test is complete.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain._create_device_for_idcode
documentation: Creates a JTAGDevice object for the relevant idcode.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.enumerate
documentation:
Initializes the JTAG TAP FSM, and attempts to identify all connected devices.

Parameters:
    return_idcodes -- If true, this method will return a list of IDCodes rather than JTAGDevice objects.

Returns a list of JTAGDevices (return_idcodes=False) or JTAG IDCODES (return_idcodes=True).

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.play_svf_instructions
documentation:
Executes a string of JTAG SVF instructions, strumming the relevant scan chain.

svf_string   -- A string containing valid JTAG SVF instructions to be executed.
log_function -- If provided, this function will be called with verbose operation information.
log_error    -- This function will be used to print information about errors that occur.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.play_svf_file
documentation:
Executes the JTAG SVF instructions from the given file.

svf_file     -- A filename or file object pointing to a JTAG SVF file.
log_function -- If provided, this function will be called with verbose operation information.
log_error    -- This function will be used to print information about errors that occur.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.jtag.JTAGChain.force_bitbang_mode
documentation: Controls whether we're forcing our device to communicate using bitbang mode.

classes.name: apollo_fpga.protocol.jtag_svf.SVFLexer

methods.name: apollo_fpga.protocol.jtag_svf.SVFLexer.line_column
documentation:
Return a `(line, column)` tuple for the given or, if not specified, current position.

Both the line and the column start at 1.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.protocol.jtag_svf.SVFLexer.peek
documentation: Return the next token without advancing the position.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.protocol.jtag_svf.SVFLexer.next
documentation: Return the next token and advance the position.

A Serial Vector Format lexer.

Comments (`! comment`, `// comment`) are ignored.

The following tokens are recognized:
: - Keyword (`HIR`, `SIR`, `TIO`, ..., `;`), returned as Python `str`;
  - Integer (`8`, `16`, ...), returned as Python `int`;
  - Real (`1E0`, `1E+0`, `1E-0`, ...), returned as Python `float`;
  - Bit array (`(0)`, `(1234)`, `(F00F)`, ...), returned as Python `bits`;
  - Literal (`(HLUDXZHHLL)`, `(IN FOO)`, ...), returned as Python `tuple(str,)`;
  - End of file, returned as Python `None`.

```
type buffer

str

attr buffer

Input buffer.

type position

int

attr position

Offset into buffer from which the next token will be read.
```

classes.name: apollo_fpga.protocol.jtag_svf.SVFParser
documentation:
A Serial Vector Format streaming parser.

This parser maintains and allows querying lexical state (e.g. "sticky" `TDI` is
automatically tracked), and invokes the SVF event handler for all commands so that
any necessary action may be taken.

methods.name: apollo_fpga.protocol.jtag_svf.SVFEventHandler.svf_pio
documentation: Called when the `PIO` command is encountered.

An abstract base class for Serial Vector Format parsing events.

The methods of this class are called when a well-formed SVF command is encountered.
The parser takes care of maintaining all lexical state (e.g. "sticky" parameters),
but all logical state is maintained by the event handler.
